flags.concern: Agent chat queue error recovery: the plan's drain effect removes the head queued item before attempting `sendNow`, but the current send path only clears `optimisticMessage` when a matching real turn arrives. If a queued send fails, that item disappears from the queue and the stale optimistic state blocks further draining, so the plan's stated 'continue draining after an error' behavior will not actually work.
flags.evidence: In `src/tools/video-editor/components/AgentChat/AgentChat.tsx:375-416`, `optimisticMessage` is set before `mutateAsync()` and is only cleared by the effect that finds a matching persisted user turn; the `finally` block does not clear it on error. In `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useAgentSession.ts:348-354`, mutation failure only sets `localError`. Step 4's proposed effect pops `[next, ...rest]` with `setQueue(rest)` before `sendNow(...)`, so a failed queued send is lost and the `optimisticMessage` guard in the same step would keep the remaining queue from advancing.

flags.concern: Agent chat optimistic completion signal: the plan relies on `optimisticMessage` clearing as a reliable signal that the previous queued turn has been reflected, but the current implementation matches only on `role === 'user' && content === optimisticMessage.text`. Two queued messages with the same text will satisfy that check against the first persisted turn, so the next queued item can auto-send too early.
flags.evidence: The existing matcher in `src/tools/video-editor/components/AgentChat/AgentChat.tsx:375-384` ignores timestamp and attachments and clears optimism when any user turn has the same `content`. `AgentTurn` already carries a `timestamp` field in `src/tools/video-editor/types/agent-session.ts:28-35`, but Step 4 still treats the current text-only optimistic clear as a reliable drain gate. Repeated prompts like 'ok' or 'continue' are enough to break that assumption.
